markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' November 2026 delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19 for additional polish has extended the window before release, shifting trader focus toward cultural and tech milestones that could occur in the interim. Development began years earlier under Take-Two Interactive, with prior postponements from a 2025 target reflecting the scale of the open-world project and quality priorities. This timeline creates opportunities for outcomes like major album drops, AI advancements, or market milestones to resolve ahead of the highly anticipated title, which historically dominates gaming attention upon launch. Traders monitor Rockstar's ongoing updates and industry benchmarks as key signals in this extended period.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial, including the May 2026 deadlock on the Jessica Mann rape charge, have reinforced trader expectations that additional prison time beyond his existing California 16-year sentence remains unlikely. At 74, with roughly six years already served at Rikers and ongoing appeals in both states, the market assigns overwhelming implied probability to no further incarceration due to health considerations, sentencing precedents for elderly defendants, and prosecutorial challenges in securing unanimous verdicts. A June 2026 hearing on potential further retrial looms as the next key catalyst, though historical patterns in prolonged #MeToo cases favor resolutions that limit extended time behind bars.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Vegas Golden Knights and Carolina Hurricanes has shaped trader consensus, with Vegas at 62% implied probability reflecting its dominant Western Conference run, including a sweep of the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche, and a stifling defensive playoff performance. Carolina reached the series after dropping just one postseason game overall, bolstered by strong regular-season standings and Eastern Conference dominance, yet the split results in the first two Final games—Vegas winning Game 1 on the road before falling in overtime in Game 2—have kept the matchup tightly contested. Home-ice advantage for Vegas in Game 3 and both clubs' proven postseason experience continue to influence positioning ahead of the remaining best-of-seven series.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a commanding 2-0 lead in the 2026 NBA Finals after road victories in Games 1 and 2, shifting trader consensus toward their 62.5% implied probability as they chase a first title since 1973. The San Antonio Spurs, appearing in their first Finals since 2013-14 with a young core anchored by Victor Wembanyama, saw their odds drop to 37.3% following those results despite entering as series favorites. Upcoming home games at Madison Square Garden for New York provide a rest and crowd advantage in a matchup featuring contrasting styles, with the Knicks' recent playoff momentum and roster depth cited in series analyses as key differentiators. Resolution hinges on the best-of-seven outcome.

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The close clustering of implied probabilities among France, Spain, Portugal, and England stems from the expanded 48-team format and parity across elite European squads entering the June 11 kickoff. Recent friendlies and final roster confirmations highlight fitness concerns, including Spain's Lamine Yamal doubts for the opener, Netherlands defender Jurriën Timber ruled out with a groin issue, and multiple Brazilian absences that have tempered expectations for South American sides. FIFA rankings place Argentina, Spain, and France near the top, yet no single nation shows overwhelming momentum or depth advantages. This balance, combined with historical tournament volatility and strong depth across several contenders, sustains the tight trader consensus reflected in current pricing.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.8% amid a wide-open field where no major candidates have formally declared. Traders assign him the highest implied probability due to his national profile as California governor, frequent media appearances criticizing the Trump administration, and recent moves toward populist positions on issues like AI regulation. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez follows at 9.0% as she conducts a national tour and tests progressive support, while Kamala Harris sits at 7.8% on residual name recognition. The fragmented field, with no contender above 24%, reflects early positioning ahead of primaries, where factors such as Southern voter outreach, Senate confirmation dynamics, and general-election electability in swing states could consolidate support. Upcoming midterm results and any formal announcements may shift these trader assessments.
